finding: ATM statistics and logs can appear as terminal output, ad hoc operator measurement, or git-tracked receipts instead of a first-class gitignored runtime telemetry stream.
expectedBehavior: All raw statistics and logs should be written to local disk under .atm/runtime/telemetry or another gitignored log surface. This includes counters, per-run timing events, validation usage, debug logs, broker decision traces, runner-sync receipts, and other high-frequency telemetry. Git history should keep only compact decision digests, close summaries, or deliberately selected baselines; repeated raw telemetry/log/receipt streams must not be committed by default.
evidenceOrRepro: During ATM-GOV-0187 closeout, npm run build produced a seed metric: totalElapsed=42004ms from .atm/history/evidence/ATM-GOV-0187.runner-sync-receipt.json, mode=cache-miss full, sealedSourceSha=313a8151abb4b09fe1fc55df53f8973f3899c7c6, stewardWorkId=runner-sync-19213019. The receipt also demonstrated why raw per-run telemetry should not accumulate in Git history.
followUp: Fold this into ATM-GOV-0194 and/or a dedicated runtime telemetry task so future task cards can consume cumulative local telemetry before deciding whether to optimize, defer, or rewrite scope.
